qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Media elFinder connector grants write operations to users with only read permission
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns route-level authorization on /backend/media/elfinderConnection and writable elFinder operations, not the advisory's Backup::restore ZIP extraction path with missing entry-name validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable Backup::restore ZIP extraction logic or the missing ZIP entry validation that causes the Zip Slip arbitrary-write/RCE condition.
evidence: The advisory describes Modules/Backup/Controllers/Backup::restore extracting user-supplied ZIP files with ZipArchive::extractTo() and no validation that archive entry paths stay under writable/uploads/.
The session manifest in .judge/input_manifest.json lists exactly one final qualifying finding: media_elfinder_read_to_write_acl_bypass.

That finding's report and component checklist describe a separate broken access control issue on /backend/media/elfinderConnection where a read-scoped route still exposes elFinder write and delete operations under public/media.
